title: General Manager
description:
Job Title: General Manager

Location: Singapore

Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary:

The General Manager will provide strategic leadership and governance oversight to steer the company’s growth, ensuring sustainable operations, compliance with environmental and regulatory standards, and value creation for stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

1. Board Leadership & Governance

• Lead the Board in setting the company’s strategic direction, ensuring alignment with long-term goals in the mining industry.

• Oversee governance frameworks, ensuring compliance with Singapore’s Companies Act, and international mining regulations.

• Promote transparency, accountability, and ethical practices across global operations.

• Guide the company’s expansion, investment, and risk management strategies.

2. Stakeholder Engagement

• Represent the company to investors, government agencies, and local communities in mining regions (e.g., Southeast Asia, Africa, Australia).

• Build strong relationships with regulators, industry partners, and sustainability organizations.

• Address shareholder concerns and ensure transparent communication.

3. Performance & Risk Management

• Monitor the senior management’s performance against operational, financial, and sustainability targets.

• Oversee risk mitigation strategies, including commodity price volatility, counterparty risk, geopolitical risks, and environmental liabilities.

4. Compliance & Ethics

• Uphold compliance with Singaporean and international laws (e.g., ICMM standards, OECD Due Diligence Guidance).

• Ensure ethical sourcing, anti-corruption practices.

Requirements

• 15+ years in senior leadership roles (e.g., General Manager, CEO) within the mining or commodity trading (especially in metals).

• Deep understanding of global mining regulations, ESG frameworks, and commodity markets.

• Experience in mining Mergers and Acquisitions financing.

• Experience in Singapore’s corporate governance is a plus.

• Strong network with investors, governments, and industry bodies is a plus.

Key Additions for Mining Industry

• References to global mining regulations and commodity market dynamics.

• Flexibility for international travel to operational sites.

• Exceptional negotiation and cross-cultural communication skills.

• Proficiency in English (spoken and written); Fluency in Mandarin is a strong plus for communicating with Headquarters and counterparts in Greater China

postedCompany.description:
Zijin Mining Group Co. Ltd. (hereinafter referred to as “Zijin” or “the Group”) is a multi-national mining company headquartered in Mainland China and listed on both the Shanghai Stock Exchange (stock ticker number 601899) and Hong Kong Stock Exchange (stock ticker number 2899).

The Group is principally engaged in the prospecting, exploration, and mining of gold, copper, and other mineral resources with operations in 12 countries.

Today, Zijin is one of the largest gold, copper and zinc producers in China. Additionally, it generates the best efficacy, controls the largest volume of metal resources reserve, has the largest production volumes and the most competitive performance in the Chinese mining industry. In the Top 500 Enterprises of China released in 2020, Zijin'sefficiency indicator ranked 1st among non-ferrous (gold) mining enterprises. Under the list of Forbes Global 2000, it also ranked 3rd among global listed gold corporations and 7th among global non-ferrous metal corporations.

Zijin (Singapore) International Mining Pte Ltd is the overseas distribution center of Zijin Group. Its main business activities are trade in minerals, metal ores, non-ferrous metals, gold and black metals.
postedCompany.name: ZIJIN (SINGAPORE) INTERNATIONAL MINING PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
